blocks.text: went in. The detail about Dean sat quietly in a way that most details don't.
didn't follow it. Not yet. But it didn't disappear the way small things usually do.
went back to his rental. He hoped Marcus would find whatever it was before Sunday.
sat with Marcus another minute. Then went inside.

blocks.speaker: Marcus Calloway
blocks.content: Hey. You good?

blocks.speaker: Nadia Reyes
blocks.content: Of course. Long drive, that's all.

blocks.speaker: Marcus Calloway
blocks.content: You've been quiet since you got in, though. That's different from tired.

blocks.speaker: Nadia Reyes
blocks.content: You know how I am. I'm the one who listens. That's always been how I am in a group.

blocks.speaker: Marcus Calloway
blocks.content: I've known you for twelve years. I know the difference between you listening and you being somewhere else.

blocks.speaker: Nadia Reyes
blocks.content: I'm right here, Marcus.

blocks.speaker: Marcus Calloway
blocks.content: Okay.

blocks.speaker: Nadia Reyes
blocks.content: I'm here. I wanted to be here. You organized this whole thing and it's beautiful and the fire was good. I'm just tired.

blocks.speaker: Marcus Calloway
blocks.content: I believe you. I'm not pushing. I just — I check. You know I check.

blocks.speaker: Nadia Reyes
blocks.content: You always check.

blocks.speaker: Marcus Calloway
blocks.content: Somebody has to. Come sit closer to the fire for ten more minutes. Then you can be tired all you want.

blocks.speaker: Nadia Reyes
blocks.content: In a minute.

blocks.speaker: Marcus Calloway
blocks.content: You've been saying that for half an hour.

blocks.speaker: Nadia Reyes
blocks.content: Then I'm almost there.

blocks.speaker: Marcus Calloway
blocks.content: You're impossible.

blocks.speaker: Nadia Reyes
blocks.content: You keep coming back anyway.

blocks.speaker: Marcus Calloway
blocks.content: Every time.

blocks.speaker: Marcus Calloway
blocks.content: I'm glad you came this weekend. For real.

blocks.speaker: Nadia Reyes
blocks.content: Me too.

She smiled when she said it. I'd been watching her long enough to know what her baseline looked like. This read softer than that. Easier. Which meant either she was being genuine or she knew exactly how genuine looks.
She smiled when she said it. It looked like she meant it. Both things could be true.

blocks.prompt: Marcus cares about her and she knows it. What does Julian read in that?

options.label: She meant it. Whatever she's carrying, she meant she was glad to be here.
She said exactly what he needed to hear. That's a different thing from just saying it.
He stops watching. He'd been doing enough of that tonight.
